aliases: Coffee with condensed milk less sugar

english: Coffee with condensed milk less sugar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general coffee beverage class; no specific entry for Singaporean kopi with condensed milk and reduced sugar. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers coffee as consumed; no specific code for kopi siu dai with condensed milk. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 covers coffee as a substance; no specific code for prepared kopi siu dai beverage. Closest parent match used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard kopitiam cup of kopi siu dai is approximately 200 ml (200 g), served hot in a ceramic cup or glass.

nutrient_source.title: Kopi Siu Dai — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists Kopi Siu Dai as a named entry with per-100g nutrient values. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken directly from this source. Confidence 0.95 as reported in input record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for sweetened milky coffee
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for kopi siu dai in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 55 is estimated by analogy to sweetened milky coffee beverages (condensed milk + sugar + coffee) documented in the Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI tables. GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low category (low: ≤55) per the University of Sydney classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 20.46) / 100 ≈ 11.3 for a 200 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar from condensed milk (7.09 g/100 g) exceeds the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold; independent association between sugar-sweetened beverages and elevated blood pressure documented in AHA 2018 guidelines.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid kopi siu dai and switch to kopi O kosong or kopi C kosong to eliminate sugar-driven blood pressure risk; sodium at 25 mg/100 g is low but the sugar load independently elevates cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.09 g sugar/100 g and negligible dietary fibre (0.18 g/100 g); liquid sugar from condensed milk is rapidly absorbed, causing acute postprandial glucose excursions.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid kopi siu dai entirely; the combination of condensed milk sugar and minimal fibre produces rapid glycaemic spikes — kopi O kosong or kopi C kosong are the recommended diabetes-safe alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from condensed milk (~1.1 g/100 g) and added sugar (7.09 g/100 g) contribute to dyslipidaemia and endothelial inflammation risk with regular consumption of multiple cups per day.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, limit kopi siu dai to occasional consumption and prefer unsweetened coffee alternatives; the saturated fat and sugar combination from condensed milk elevates LDL and triglyceride risk with habitual intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar from condensed milk promotes pro-inflammatory mediators and negates the anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit of coffee.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should switch to kopi O kosong or kopi C kosong to preserve coffee's anti-inflammatory benefits while eliminating the pro-inflammatory sugar load from condensed milk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kopi Siu Dai (咖啡少甜, literally 'coffee less sweet') is a traditional Singaporean kopitiam coffee prepared with robusta coffee beans roasted with butter and sugar, brewed through a cloth sock strainer, and served with a reduced amount of sweetened condensed milk compared to standard kopi. The 'siu dai' modifier (from Cantonese 少甜, meaning 'less sweet') signals to the hawker to add less condensed milk than the default portion. Per 100 g, it provides 60 kcal, 0.7 g protein, 1.84 g fat (of which approximately 1.1 g is saturated from condensed milk), 10.23 g carbohydrate, and 7.09 g sugar, with only 0.18 g dietary fibre and 25 mg sodium. Despite the 'less sweet' label, the sugar content at 7.09 g/100 g still classifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under Singapore HPB and WHO criteria (>5 g/100 g). The glycaemic index is estimated at 55 (low, at the upper boundary of the low category) by analogy to sweetened milky coffee, with a glycaemic load of approximately 11.3 per standard 200 ml cup. Clinically, kopi siu dai is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes or hypertension due to its SSB sugar load and rapid glucose absorption profile. The beverage is a staple of Singapore breakfast culture, commonly consumed at kopitiams (traditional coffee shops) alongside kaya toast and soft-boiled eggs. It is halal-certified in most kopitiam settings and is vegetarian but not vegan due to condensed milk.

Kopi siu dai vs kopi O kosong — which is healthier?
Is kopi siu dai a sugar-sweetened beverage?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95 as an exact named entry. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened milky coffee, reducing GI confidence to medium for that sub-field only. Overall record confidence is high given exact HPB source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data for kopi siu dai are not individually reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this beverage as of 2026; these fields are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo source consulted. Potassium estimated at 75 mg/100 g based on typical milky coffee composition from USDA FoodData Central (coffee with milk, USDA SR Legacy) as a supporting reference; this is an estimate and should be verified against a future HPB update. Saturated fat estimated at 1.1 g/100 g based on condensed milk contribution consistent with HPB food composition data for similar kopi variants. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate — no direct GI measurement for kopi siu dai exists in the University of Sydney GI Database; the estimate is based on sweetened milky coffee analogues. GI 55 is classified as low (≤55) per University of Sydney GI classification; gi_category set to 'low' accordingly. Despite the 'siu dai' (less sweet) modifier, sugar at 7.09 g/100 g still exceeds the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold under HPB and WHO definitions.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Documented all -1 fields in 'notes' as required by g3_numeric_hygiene hard gate.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match_type values to 'closest_parent'. Added Tamil and Hindi aliases to flat aliases array. Updated gi_category to 'medium' consistent with GI 55.

Iteration 3: Fixed g3_numeric_hygiene — expanded notes to explicitly state that all -1 fields are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo source consulted, providing the missing-data basis documentation for every -1 sentinel value. Fixed q4_glycemic_completeness — corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' since GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low category (≤55) per University of Sydney GI classification; updated gi_source notes and llm description accordingly.
